Astrobotic showed off the lunar lander it plans to launch later this year that will be the vanguard of NASA

summary:
Katalyst Space raises $12 million for GEO servicing demo mission 
Satellite servicing startup Katalyst Space Technologies has raised $12 million for a

title: PiLogic partners with Air Force lab to test satellite fault-prediction software
summary: PiLogic has partnered with an Air Force lab to test its satellite fault-prediction software. The startup employs probabilistic reasoning to diagnose spacecraft anomalies.

title: A UN agency that works for US space
summary:
A UN agency that works for US space 
If United States policymakers deride the wider United Nations, they still spend on the International Telecommunic

title: Geospatial industry launches maritime initiative
summary: The geospatial industry has launched a new maritime initiative to address rising demand from defense agencies and commercial shipping sectors for enhanced maritime domain awareness technologies.

title: Deep Space Network antenna mishap blamed on poor training and procedures
summary: A NASA investigation found that poor training and procedures caused millions of dollars in damage to a Deep Space Network (DSN) antenna. The mishap highlights systemic issues in operational protocols.

title: Tianwen-2 makes series of burns on approach to asteroid, according to radio tracking
summary: China's Tianwen-2 spacecraft conducted multiple small propulsive maneuvers after a main burn on June 7, preparing for an asteroid rendezvous in July. The mission's progress was confirmed through radio tracking data.

title: NASA X-ray spacecraft catches jet erupting from 1st supermassive black hole imaged by humanity
summary: NASA's X-ray spacecraft has captured a detailed image of a jet erupting from the first supermassive black hole ever imaged by humanity. This unprecedented observation reveals changes in the jet with high-resolution X-ray data.

title: Watch European rocket launch record-breaking mission for Amazon on June 17
summary: An Ariane 6 heavy-lift rocket will launch a record-breaking payload for Amazon on June 17. The event can be watched live.

title: How to see the moon hide Venus in a rare daytime sky show on June 17
summary: A rare daytime occultation will occur on June 17, where a thin crescent moon will pass directly in front of Venus. This celestial event will be visible to observers in certain regions.

title: The Nikon Z fc is our choice as the best budget camera — this retro-inspired beauty is at its best price of the year in this early Amazon Prime Day deal
summary: Nikon Z fc is highlighted as the best budget camera with a retro design, currently at its lowest price of the year during an early Amazon Prime Day deal.

title: Watch a fireball burn a 300 mile path above the Midwest US (video)
summary: A fireball streaked across the Midwest US, traveling 300 miles in seconds before disintegrating. The event was captured on video.

title: Don't miss the moon lead a dazzling mini 'planet parade' on June 17
summary: On June 17, the crescent moon will align with Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury in a mini 'planet parade' while passing through the Beehive open star cluster. This celestial event offers a rare visual spectacle for stargazers.

title: SpaceX IPO brings Starship to NYC | Space photo of the day for June 16, 2026
summary: SpaceX's IPO on Nasdaq made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire, coinciding with the debut of Starship in NYC. The event highlights SpaceX's financial milestone and public visibility.

title: Move over 'Mass Effect': 'Exodus' just got an extended gameplay reveal, and we are beyond hyped for this sci-fi RPG
summary: A new sci-fi RPG titled 'Exodus' has released an extended gameplay reveal, showcasing its immersive universe and gameplay mechanics. Players are invited to explore the Centauri Cluster and the planet Sang in this highly anticipated title.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 3 huge BlueBird direct-to-cell satellites from Florida early on June 17
summary: SpaceX is set to launch three AST SpaceMobile BlueBird direct-to-cell satellites using a Falcon 9 rocket from Florida on June 17. The event can be watched live.

title: James Webb Space Telescope forecasts extreme weather on exoplanet that rains rubies and sapphires
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has provided detailed observations of an exoplanet where extreme weather conditions include raining rubies and sapphires. This marks a significant advancement in exoplanet atmospheric studies.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus has shifted after the completion of Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, and LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13, following the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and multiple Starlink missions from different locations. Key flights are scheduled from China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month struggle to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on long-term goals despite the setback.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on June 2 at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and select materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test ensures the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-m diameter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars or the Solar System besides Earth, will aid the rover's landing in 2028 to search for signs of life.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace secures $25M in Series B funding to advance its Aurora spaceplane and demonstrate in-space refueling. The New Zealand-Dutch company aims to accelerate development with the new capital.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data storage.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ The funding round comes amid the UK government’s full-court press to expand its sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-driven anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a decline in stocks of other publicly traded space companies, causing a broad pullback among investors. Major players in the space industry saw significant drops following SpaceX's strong market debut.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry. The move could shift reliance from private capital to public markets, benefiting other companies in the sector.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the type of economy that will emerge. The focus is on whether this capital will drive operational growth or merely accumulate hardware.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a significant event for the global space industry, with major implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape competition and investment dynamics in the industry.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox launched an AI-focused update to enhance the effectiveness of AI agents as geospatial-data analysts. This update aims to improve data analysis capabilities in the space industry.

title: Eutelsat Secures First Order Under French Defense Agreement
summary: Eutelsat has secured its first order under a new agreement with the French Ministry of the Armed Forces and Veterans. The deal includes provisions for Low-Earth Orbit capabilities.

title: All.Space Relocates to US Amid York Space Acquisition Process
summary: All.Space has relocated from the U.K. to Alabama as part of its acquisition process by York Space Systems, aligning with U.S. defense priorities.

title: Arianespace Readies Record Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: Arianespace is preparing for a record-breaking mission to launch the heaviest payload by an Ariane rocket and the most Amazon Leo satellites in a single deployment. This marks a significant milestone for both Arianespace and Amazon's satellite constellation efforts.

title: Anuvu Names Former HBO Exec as CEO
summary: Anuvu has appointed former HBO executive Simon Sutton as its new CEO, replacing Josh Marks. The announcement was made on June 15.

title: Gilat to Acquire Comtech’s Satellite and Space Business, Targeting US Defense Growth
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks is acquiring Comtech’s Satellite and Space Communications segment to strengthen its position in the U.S. defense market. The deal combines two major players in satellite ground technology.

title: Israel’s El Al Airline to Adopt Starlink for IFC
summary: Israel's El Al Airline will adopt SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight connectivity, with service to be rolled out gradually. The agreement marks a significant expansion of Starlink's aviation services.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price of $135, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is emerging as critical infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge. This integration is becoming essential for enterprises managing remote assets and industrial operations.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to leverage Starlink's satellite internet for global emergency preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded three Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its EO capabilities.
